title: psacalium pachyphyllum
intro: Psacalium pachyphyllum, also known as treemazate or Mexican tree daisy, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Psacalium pachyphyllum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional herbal medicine, Psacalium pachyphyllum can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
